markdown:
# Rendering Guide rgd.hcc.001

- Project: Hepatocellular carcinoma starter project
- Workflow run: run.hcc.001
- Disease: Hepatocellular carcinoma
- Generated at: 2026-04-23T15:00:00Z
- Providers: nano-banana-pro, genspark-ai-slides

## Run Summary
- One sentence: Hepatocellular carcinoma is a primary liver malignancy that often emerges from chronically injured tissue and should be discovered through fair clues.
- Patient experience summary: The patient notices vague but escalating symptoms that only make sense once the detectives investigate the hidden liver lesion.
- Key mechanism: Malignant hepatocyte growth distorts local architecture inside a chronically damaged liver.
- Time scale: months to years on top of chronic liver disease
- Audience tier: provider-education
- Style profile: whimsical-mystery
- Educational focus: liver physiology; tumor biology; diagnostic markers

## Franchise Rules
- Mystery first: observation and suspicion should lead before explicit naming.
- Diagnosis must be earned through clues, not front-loaded as a title card.
- Treatment is the action climax and should resolve the mechanism uncovered in the reveal.
- The wrap-up must echo the opener so the franchise loop closes cleanly.
- Keep art and lettering separate. Do not place large visible text inside generated image prompts.

## Continuity Bible
- Continuity anchors: same two recurring alien detectives; miniature jet packs; case tablet hologram; red-gold liver environment
- Character locks: Detective A is shorter and more impulsive; Detective B is taller and more analytical
- Anatomy locks: liver-inspired tissue geometry; focal abnormal district contrasts with surrounding tissue; avoid generic intestine-like visuals
- Style locks: action-packed whimsical comic mystery; high clarity; panel composition optimized for later lettering
- Lettering policy: Never request visible dialogue, captions, labels, or teaching copy inside the generated art. Apply all text from the separate lettering layer after image generation or slide placement.

## Global Negative Constraints
- no speech bubbles
- no captions
- no labels
- no medical chart overlays
- no gore

## Genspark Deck Bootstrap Prompt
```
Create a slide deck for the run "run.hcc.001" from the attached rendering guide "rgd.hcc.001". Use exactly 1 slides, with one comic panel per slide and no parallel slide generation. Build the outline first, then create slides sequentially. Lock slide 1 style and require every later slide to match it. Use only the supplied panel content for Hepatocellular carcinoma. Do not replace the medical content with live web research or new claims. Keep all visible text editable and sourced from the provided lettering entries, not embedded in the art. The final deck should preserve the project title "Hepatocellular carcinoma starter project" and remain clinically traceable to the supplied claim references.
```

## Retry Guidance
- If a panel loses anatomy clarity, simplify the composition before weakening the medical objective.
- If character continuity drifts, explicitly restate the detective locks and reuse the first successful slide or image as the style reference.
- If the model adds visible text, remove all requested text from the art prompt and keep overlays in the lettering instructions only.
- If Genspark drifts slide style across the deck, regenerate sequentially and lock slide 1 as the reference for every remaining slide.

## Panel 9.3 — pnl.hcc.004.03

- Scene: scn.hcc.004
- Story function: first major clue
- Beat goal: Reveal a suspicious focal region worth investigating.
- Medical objective: Suggest autonomous abnormal growth without naming the diagnosis.
- Location: adjacent to suspicious liver region
- Body scale: tissue
- Action summary: They hover near a district of cells whose architecture breaks the surrounding pattern.
- Camera framing: medium shot with focal push-in
- Camera angle: slightly canted
- Composition notes: Contrast the suspicious region against more orderly surrounding tissue.
- Lighting mood: ominous discovery
- Clue revealed: A focal abnormal region behaves like a hidden stronghold within the liver.
- Continuity anchors: case tablet hologram; jet pack glow; distorted tissue geometry
- Acceptance checks: focal lesion implied; no explicit diagnosis label in art; clear contrast between normal and abnormal tissue

### Claim References
- clm.hcc.002: HCC reflects malignant transformation of hepatocytes. (Approved oncology review)
- clm.hcc.003: Tumor growth changes local perfusion and tissue architecture. (Approved radiology review)

### Lettering Overlay
- dialogue / Detective B: "That district is rewriting the neighborhood. Follow it, but do not name it yet." at right-side stacked balloons
